Headline: Closure - Woodcote House, Surrey
Info: A newly published retrospective highlights the permanent closure of the 150-year-old independent preparatory school, underscoring a broader trend that saw 45 prep schools close over the past year due to severe financial headwinds and VAT policies.

Headline: Closure - Shaugh Prior Primary School (State), Devon
Info: Devon County Council has issued a statutory proposal to permanently close the rural primary school, which currently has only two pupils, due to long-term educational and financial unsustainability.

Headline: Closure - Kings Education Beckenham, London
Info: Kings Education has announced the permanent closure of its Beckenham campus at the end of the current term, citing external financial pressures and a decline in enrolments exacerbated by the introduction of VAT on private school fees.

Headline: Merger - Independent Schools Sector, England and Wales
Info: The latest Good Merger Index reports a record spike in charity mergers, driven predominantly by the consolidation of the independent school sector as institutions face VAT changes, cost pressures, and declining fee income.

Headline: Financial - Isle of Wight Primary Schools (State), Isle of Wight
Info: A new council report warns that 23 maintained primary schools on the island are forecast to run financial deficits by the 2026/27 academic year, making the reduction of school places and potential closures a critical priority.

Headline: Merger - Saltney Ferry Primary School (State School), Flintshire
Info: Flintshire County Council has temporarily deferred a £12.5 million plan to merge Saltney Ferry Primary School with Saltney Wood Memorial Primary School following a late legal challenge. The controversial proposal, which would result in the closure of Saltney Ferry, faced significant opposition with over 3,000 people objecting to the plans.

Headline: Closure - Brabyns Preparatory School (Independent), Stockport
Info: The Bellevue Education group has launched a formal consultation to close this 86-pupil co-educational prep school by the end of the summer term. The proposal follows a prolonged review of the ongoing financial challenges facing the independent school sector, though the school's nursery will remain open.

Headline: Closure - Dean Hole Church of England Primary School (State School), Caunton
Info: Nottinghamshire County Council has confirmed this rural village school will permanently close on 31 August 2026 due to significant financial and operational difficulties. The school currently has only eight pupils and was forecast to face a budget deficit of around £100,000 by August 2026.

Headline: Closure - Prestwich Preparatory School (Independent), Prestwich
Info: This £8,000-a-year independent school will permanently close on 27 March 2026, leaving parents with just three weeks to find alternative arrangements. The closure is attributed to the headteacher's ill health and increased financial pressures including VAT, with pupil numbers having fallen to just 36.

Headline: Closure - Kings London (Independent), Beckenham
Info: Kings Education has announced the permanent closure of its London Beckenham college, effective 20 March 2026, as part of a strategic UK restructuring. The provider cited the introduction of VAT on independent school fees as a key factor causing a decline in student enrolments.
